lore.tradition: Ojibwe/Cree/Algonquian Windigo; Eastern Woodland Underground Panther (Mishipeshu)
lore.ecology: cave predator, large quadruped, pack hunter with thermal seniority hierarchy
lore.scale_mode: physiological expansion under starvation/cold/stress — NOT evil transformation. The Windigo silhouette.

base_description: large quadruped predator, pale white-blue winter coloration, huge reflective cave-adapted eyes glowing in darkness, thick fur, heavy predator build, cave dweller, winter cave hunter

emotion_states.note: resting, eyes half-open scanning, energy conservation
pack-greeting: body lowered, ears up, social signal
pre-territorial display, fur bristling, warning before Scale Mode
freeze-then-flee: belly near ground, eyes wide, panic
cold conservation, stillness, eyes tracking without body motion
head down over prey, eyes up scanning, protective feeding

lifecycle.note: deep cave rest, curled, heat conservation posture

scale_mode.note: Windigo silhouette: fur bristled, expanded size
Windigo silhouette: fur bristled, expanded size

cultural_note: Do NOT frame Scale Mode as evil or pathological. The Windigo in Ojibwe/Cree/Algonquian tradition is a BEING/FORCE (consequence of starvation and cold), not a mental illness. The Ven'thari are a creature. Scale Mode is a metabolic survival response.
pending: PixelLab character animation (walk cycle, idle loop, scale mode transition, territorial boundary stance)
